theme.title: Broad Risk-Off
theme.summary: Energy (+0.29%) and Technology (+0.13%) led the market today, while Health Care (-1.33%) and Materials (-1.28%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged -0.30%, defensives -1.04%, and value/cyclicals -0.62% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 -0.19%, Dow -0.59%, Nasdaq -0.05% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.67%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 0.48% to 18.65, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold slipped 0.47% to $4,008.90, crude oil was little changed at $82.42, and bitcoin gained 0.01% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
